evals.prompt: I need to set up Duende IdentityServer as a SAML 2.0 Identity Provider. I already have IdentityServer configured with OIDC clients. Show me how to add SAML support and register a service provider with entity ID https://crm.contoso.com that posts assertions to https://crm.contoso.com/saml/acs.
evals.expected_output: Working code that calls .AddSaml() on the IdentityServer builder, registers an in-memory SamlServiceProvider with the correct EntityId and AssertionConsumerServiceUrls, and does NOT add a separate NuGet package.
assertions: The code calls .AddSaml() on the IdentityServer builder chain
No separate NuGet package is added — SAML is built into Duende.IdentityServer
A SamlServiceProvider is created with EntityId set to 'https://crm.contoso.com'
AssertionConsumerServiceUrls contains 'https://crm.contoso.com/saml/acs'
The code mentions or uses AddInMemorySamlServiceProviders to register the SP

evals.prompt: We're going to production with our SAML IdP. Right now we use AddInMemorySamlServiceProviders. What's the recommended approach for production, and can you show me how to implement a custom store backed by EF Core?
evals.expected_output: An explanation that in-memory stores are not suitable for production, followed by a custom ISamlServiceProviderStore implementation using EF Core with FindByEntityIdAsync, registered via AddSamlServiceProviderStore<T>().
assertions: Warns against using in-memory stores in production
Implements ISamlServiceProviderStore interface
The store class has a FindByEntityIdAsync method that accepts a string entityId and CancellationToken
Uses AddSamlServiceProviderStore<T>() for registration
Uses EF Core (DbContext) for data access

evals.prompt: I have a legacy SAML service provider that expects attributes in a custom format like urn:custom:email instead of standard claim types. How can I customize the claim-to-attribute mapping in Duende IdentityServer's SAML support?
evals.expected_output: A custom ISamlClaimsMapper implementation that maps claims to SamlAttribute objects with custom Name values, registered as a transient service. Includes a warning that implementing ISamlClaimsMapper replaces all default mappings.
assertions: Implements ISamlClaimsMapper interface
The mapper returns SamlAttribute objects with custom Name values
Warns that ISamlClaimsMapper completely replaces default claim mapping — DefaultClaimMappings are not applied
Registers the custom mapper with dependency injection (e.g., AddTransient)

evals.prompt: hey, one of our SAML service providers keeps complaining about 'response has expired' errors. clock sync seems fine on our side. what settings should I tweak?
evals.expected_output: Guidance to increase the DefaultClockSkew setting in IdentityServerOptions.Saml, with code showing how to configure it. May also mention DefaultRequestMaxAge.
assertions: Mentions DefaultClockSkew as the relevant setting
Shows configuration via options.Saml.DefaultClockSkew
Suggests increasing the value beyond the default 5 minutes
Does NOT suggest changes to the OIDC/OAuth configuration — this is SAML-specific

evals.prompt: Configure a SAML service provider in Duende IdentityServer that requires encrypted assertions. The SP's encryption certificate is at certs/sp-encryption.cer. Also enable IdP-initiated SSO for this SP.
evals.expected_output: A SamlServiceProvider configuration with EncryptAssertions = true, EncryptionCertificates populated from the certificate file, and AllowIdpInitiated = true. Should also mention enabling the IdP-initiated SSO endpoint.
assertions: Sets EncryptAssertions = true on the SamlServiceProvider
Populates EncryptionCertificates with a certificate loaded from certs/sp-encryption.cer
Sets AllowIdpInitiated = true on the SamlServiceProvider
Mentions that the IdP-initiated SSO endpoint must be enabled via options.Endpoints.EnableSamlIdpInitiatedSsoEndpoint = true
Does NOT set EncryptAssertions = true without providing EncryptionCertificates

evals.prompt: What SAML signing behaviors does Duende IdentityServer support? We have an SP that requires both the assertion and the full response to be signed. What should we use?
evals.expected_output: An explanation of all four signing behaviors (SignAssertion, SignResponse, SignBoth, DoNotSign) with a recommendation to use SignBoth for this specific case. Should note that SignAssertion is the default and most interoperable, and DoNotSign should never be used in production.
assertions: Lists all four signing behaviors: SignAssertion, SignResponse, SignBoth, DoNotSign
Recommends SignBoth for the specific requirement of signing both assertion and response
Notes that SignAssertion is the default and most interoperable option
Warns that DoNotSign should only be used for testing, never in production
Shows how to set SigningBehavior on the SamlServiceProvider or as a default via options.Saml.DefaultSigningBehavior

evals.prompt: I want to add SAML support to my IdentityServer. Do I need to install a separate NuGet package? Also, what license edition do I need?
evals.expected_output: Clear statement that SAML is built into Duende.IdentityServer (no separate package) and requires the Enterprise Edition license. Should mention this was introduced in v8.0.
assertions: States that no separate NuGet package is needed — SAML is built into Duende.IdentityServer
States that Enterprise Edition license is required
Mentions SAML support was introduced in version 8.0

evals.prompt: I'm building a login page for my IdentityServer that handles both OIDC and SAML requests. How can I detect if an incoming authentication request is a SAML AuthnRequest, and get details about the requesting service provider?
evals.expected_output: Code using ISamlInteractionService.GetRequestContextAsync(returnUrl) injected into the login controller/page, checking if the result is non-null to detect SAML requests, with access to context.ServiceProvider and context.RequestedNameIdFormat.
assertions: Uses ISamlInteractionService injected via dependency injection
Calls GetRequestContextAsync with the returnUrl parameter
Checks if the returned context is non-null to determine if it's a SAML request
Accesses context.ServiceProvider or context.RequestedNameIdFormat for SP details
